plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-342 RP11.P00.M00.S01-RP11.P01.M08.S05 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-342 bootstraps the RP11 Time Expense Disbursement descriptor program: the program contract (contracts/time-expense-core-contract.json), the packages/time-expense package, and the rp11:time-expense-core:validate gate, covering the full RP11.P00 scope foundation cycle (M00-M10: scope inventory, acceptance gate definition, non-goal boundary, target file map, contract schema outline, ownership note, matter-first trace note, permission baseline note, audit baseline note, synthetic data policy, risk register row) and the RP11.P01 model foundation head (M00-M08: package directory layout, primary entity identifier, tenant scope field, matter trace reference, lifecycle status enum, ownership metadata, reference relationship map, field registries, state transition map, validation helper, fixture model, serialization shape, public export, model unit/invalid-reference/ownership-drift tests, Hermes model summary, Claude model review prompt, closeout handoff) as twenty descriptor-only sections over 150 planned rows, without opening time entry, rate card, expense, disbursement, or any other closed runtime surface.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-342 is a descriptor-only RP11 (Time Expense Disbursement) bootstrap pack covering 150 units (RP11.P00.M00.S01-RP11.P01.M08.S05, risk C). Independent read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) supports the pack-level claim. The pack does NOT over-claim production readiness: production_ready is false across manifest.json and command-evidence.json (status implemented_pending_review); production_ready_flag is a descriptor-verification token and Hermes production_ready_candidate is gated on coverage+foundation validity. Unit/deliverable/phase/micro counts (impl 104, contract 3, security_audit 6, ui 19, fixture 3, test 9, hermes_evidence 3, claude_review 3 = 150; P00=52, P01=98) are internally consistent across plan, manifest, contract, registry, and baseline. Grep confirmed the no-write boundary: zero writeFile/child_process/network/DB/object-storage calls in packages/time-expense; the no-write attestation (all runtime/write flags false, claude not promoted, no enterprise trust from local validation) is mirrored consistently in registry.js, service.js, validators.js and the contract. Claude is preserved as read-only, non-final reviewer (review-request read_only + Read/Grep/Glob, claude_not_final_approver true, promotes_claude_to_final_approval false). Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic evidence-packet construction (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt false), not product runtime. Review hardening is clean: auth_preflight passed (exit 0, non-empty valid JSON), prompt within size, forbidden tools absent, summary-only correctly prohibited (Risk C + security/review deliverables), and the forbidden_review_evidence list correctly encodes the CP178+ rejections (auth failure, 0-byte stdout, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, missing fields). Tests are wired into npm test and the rp11:time-expense-core:validate gate ran green (exit 0). Two low-severity (P3) hygiene findings noted; neither affects correctness, boundaries, or the production_ready claim, so neither blocks pack or goal closeout. Pending post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are expected to be absent at this review stage and are produced/validated in the post-review gated commands before commit.

findings.title: Stale field name source_email_dms_core_pack_id carried over from email-DMS lineage
findings.details: The upstream-pack mirror field is named 'source_email_dms_core_pack_id' even though RP11's upstream program is RP10 (intake), not RP08 (email-DMS). The value (CP00-341) is correct and upstream_program_id is correctly RP10, so this is a misleading template carryover, not a provenance/correctness error. The same misnamed field appears in packages/time-expense/src/registry.js (~line 91), src/service.js (~line 20), src/validators.js (~line 186), the contract (lines 34, 2186, 2306), and is asserted in scripts/validate-rp11-time-expense-core-contract.mjs (~line 103). It is internally consistent so deepEqual contract/registry validation passes.
findings.recommendation: Rename to a program-neutral key such as 'upstream_pack_id' (or 'source_intake_core_pack_id') across registry/service/validators/contract/test in a future pack to avoid misleading lineage metadata. Non-blocking.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json lists pack-owned paths as both implementation and unrelated-preserved
findings.details: packages/time-expense/, docs/closeout-packs/cp00-342/, and docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md appear in BOTH untracked_implementation_files (lines 111-117) and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (lines 119-130) — contradictory labeling, since a path cannot be both pack implementation and unrelated. The authoritative sources are correct and unaffected: active_pack_implementation_refs (lines 5-16) unambiguously lists the pack files, and the manifest worktree_transition_policy.unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (manifest.json:6324-6332) excludes pack-owned paths. So the commit/closeout boundary is governed correctly; this is a scope-generator metadata inconsistency only.
findings.recommendation: Fix the changed-file-scope generator so untracked paths already classified as pack implementation are not also emitted into unrelated_dirty_files_preserved. Non-blocking; rely on active_pack_implementation_refs and the manifest worktree policy as authoritative.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-342 time expense core scope contract foundation descriptor pack
